title: Problem-Driven Design Patterns · Program Defense
summary: Add pricing, notifications, imports, commands, and persistence without turning patterns into ceremony. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an extensible library operations toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use strategy objects or callables when an algorithm varies independently and must be selected or tested explicitly.
Use factory functions or objects when construction choice, configuration, validation, or lifecycle must be centralized.
Wrap an incompatible external API behind the small role the application actually needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Oop Strategy Pattern evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Oop Strategy Pattern assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use strategy objects or callables when an algorithm varies independently and must be selected or tested explicitly. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: python patterns. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Oop Strategy Pattern, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of an extensible library operations toolkit while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Strategy requires one subclass for every branch even when functions suffice. This fails the Oop Strategy Pattern contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Python Tutorial Classes (Python 3.14.6) — Controls introductory class, instance, namespace, inheritance, iterator, and generator explanations.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use strategy objects or callables when an algorithm varies independently and must be selected or tested explicitly.

Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop State Pattern applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use state objects when behavior varies substantially by state and explicit transition logic outgrows simpler representations.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
